title: NOC 64320 – Tour and travel guides | GTR Immigration
description: Tour and travel guides under NOC 64320 are the cultural ambassadors of Canada’s multi-billion dollar tourism industry. Whether escorting groups through the historic streets of Old Quebec or leading adventure seekers through the Rockies, these TEER 4 professionals provide the essential expertise that defines the Canadian travel experience. For immigration, this role is a vital component of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) targeting the hospitality and tourism sectors, offering a stable pathway for those with exceptional communication skills and localized knowledge.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6432 – Tourism and amusement services occupationsNOC Code64320

duties: Tour Guides: Escort individuals or groups through cities, waterways, industrial sites, or historical landmarks. Provide cultural and historical facts and answer visitor questions.
Travel Guides: Accompany groups on vacation or business trips, ensuring itineraries are met and resolving any service or accommodation issues.
Describe points of interest and provide background information on interesting features.
May collect admission fees and sell souvenirs.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Knowledge of both official languages or an additional language is often mandatory.
Basic first aid and CPR certification may be required for outdoor or adventure-related guiding.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. The occupation is expected to benefit from the continued recovery of international group travel and the rise of "slow tourism" and "educational travel" across Canada.
labour_market_demand: The demand for tour guides is currently Favourable, particularly in peak tourism seasons (Summer and Winter). With Canada’s 2026-2028 Immigration Levels Plan focusing on regional growth, provinces like British Columbia, Alberta, and Nova Scotia are actively seeking guides to support their expanding eco-tourism and historical sectors. Bilingual (English/French) guides or those fluent in additional languages (Mandarin, Spanish, German) are in exceptionally high demand.
Dynamic Work Environment: Work outdoors or in iconic Canadian landmarks rather than a traditional office.
Networking: Interact with international travelers and local industry leaders daily.
Flexible Career: High potential for seasonal work or self-employment as a boutique tour operator.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time measure to transition 33,000 temporary workers into permanent residents. Hospitality and service sectors are expected to be high priority.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: A dedicated stream for those working in tourism and hospitality for at least 9 months.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): For workers in Nova Scotia, NB, or PEI with a qualifying job offer from a designated employer.
